title: Fall 2025 in the NC High Country (royalty free music by be sound.com). All drone flights were in compliance with local and federal laws as they did not originate from within BRP boundaries. #fall #foliage #ncwx #fallfoliage #nchighcountry #blueridgeparkway
creator: @jeremygilchristphotos (Instagram)
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- one exemplar added to the autumn/seasonal-foliage landscape subgenre bucket. A prior agent's candidate JSON proposed this URL and title/creator/date facts; per this task's step-1 requirement it was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was re-fetched live in this session via a single WebFetch call against https://www.instagram.com/reel/DP_whvCDCqI/ (prompted to extract creator/username, caption text, post date, drone/BRP/NC mentions, music attribution, and any CC/license markings; standard Instagram oEmbed was not used because Meta's public oEmbed endpoint requires an authenticated Graph API app token this session does not have, consistent with this pack's established fallback of a direct page WebFetch for Instagram sources, e.g. instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json and valsoiapg-gran-sasso-alpine.json). The WebFetch returned: creator/username 'jeremygilchristphotos'; caption verbatim 'Fall 2025 in the NC High Country (royalty free music by be sound.com). All drone flights were in compliance with local and federal laws as they did not originate from within BRP boundaries.'; hashtags #fall #foliage #ncwx #fallfoliage #nchighcountry #blueridgeparkway; post date 'October 19, 2025'; explicit drone mention with a compliance/legal note (flights did not originate from within Blue Ridge Parkway boundaries); music credited as royalty-free to 'be sound.com'; and 'no license markings visible on the page.' This independently reproduces the prior candidate's creator handle, caption content, post date, and compliance-note details field-for-field, so it is treated as confirmed by this session's own primary-source fetch, not a restatement of the earlier agent's claim. The candidate's supplied 'title' field ('Fall 2025 in the NC High Country (Blue Ridge Parkway autumn drone footage)') was a paraphrase, not the verbatim caption -- per this pack's convention for Instagram sources (using the actual caption text as 'title' since Instagram reels carry no separate title field), this record's 'title' is set to the verbatim caption plus hashtags actually returned by this session's WebFetch, not the candidate's paraphrase. No WebSearch was additionally run in this session for secondary corroboration (e.g. follower count, creator background, or any press/feature writeup) -- absence of that step is disclosed here rather than silently omitted; no follower-count/engagement signal was available for scoring calibration as a result. license_category is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': determination method is identical to every other record in this pack -- this session's WebFetch explicitly reported no Creative Commons or other license marking anywhere on the fetched page, which is treated as insufficient evidence for a permissive license per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly and explicitly confirmed otherwise). Consequently local_media_path stays null and no video/image file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. duration is left null: no runtime was surfaced by the WebFetch call and no YouTube-style oEmbed/API alternative exists for this Instagram URL in this session, so rather than estimate a plausible Reels-length runtime this field stays unset pending manual playback confirmation. award_or_showcase_provenance is left null: this is an individual creator's personal seasonal/travel drone reel with no juried contest, broadcaster, or festival attribution found anywhere in this session's research -- inventing one would be fabrication. scores (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are scores_provenance: manually_estimated placeholder judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the spec's no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning: this pack's documented contest-winning/broadcaster exemplars cluster 0.777-0.817 (e.g. skypixel-landscape.json 0.78/0.75/0.82, skypixel11_africa_unseen_ellisvanjason.json 0.8/0.8/0.85), while its unvetted personal-Instagram-reel exemplars with a confirmed specific drone model sit at 0.72-0.76 (instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json 0.74/0.73/0.72 with a confirmed DJI Mavic Air 2S; valsoiapg-gran-sasso-alpine.json 0.75/0.72/0.76 with a confirmed DJI Mavic 4 Pro). This record has neither a confirmed drone model/resolution (the caption names no hardware) nor any award/contest placement nor any follower/engagement signal, but it does document a genuinely careful, regulation-aware operator (the explicit BRP-boundary flight-compliance note is a real, disclosed signal of operator professionalism/planning, distinct from footage-quality itself) -- on balance this places it at the low end of this pack's personal-reel band, slightly at or below the two comparable Instagram exemplars given the absent hardware/resolution disclosure, hence sharpness=0.73, exposure=0.72, motion_smoothness=0.73. composite_score IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.73, exposure=0.72, motion_smoothness=0.73, composition=None) and drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights() (with no explicit version argument, resolving to this repo's current 'default-v2' profile: sharpness=exposure=motion_smoothness=composition=0.25) via '.venv/bin/python3' in this session, returning composite_score=0.7266666666666666 (rounded to 0.7267 here). Because composition's score is None (this pack's Milestone-1 Capability-1 convention), compute_composite_score's defensive 'skip any signal whose score is None' behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator despite its nonzero 0.25 weight, so the arithmetic reduces to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.73+0.72+0.73)/3 = 0.7266666666666666 -- confirmed by actually running the function this session, not hand-computed. The composite arithmetic itself is genuine and reproducible even though its three inputs are estimates rather than measurements.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: format='short-form-reel' is grounded in the source URL's own structure (https://www.instagram.com/reel/DP_whvCDCqI/ -- an Instagram '/reel/' path, Instagram's own product-defined capped-duration short-form post category), independently confirmed by this session's own WebFetch navigating to that exact URL and returning caption/creator/date content consistent with a live Reels post; this is a structural/platform fact, not a playback-derived judgment, mirroring instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json's grounding of the same field. No live playback or scrubbing of the video was attempted in this session (claude-in-chrome browser tools are available in this environment but were not invoked, matching this pack's precedent for Instagram-sourced records). The single WebFetch call this session returned only the caption, hashtags, post date, and compliance note already covered in the top-level notes field, with no text describing cut rhythm, shot count, transition style, or overall pacing, and no supplementary WebSearch was run in this session to seek secondary editorial commentary. Consequently estimated_cut_count, avg_shot_length_seconds, and transition_styles_observed are left null/empty rather than guessed -- no genuine text-provenance or playback basis exists for those three fields.
